section_name: Agreements with Other Entities for Joint Use
text:
A district may:

(1) enter into agreements with a public utility, private utility, communication system, common carrier, or transportation system for the joint use of its facilities, installations, or property inside or outside the district; and

(2) establish:

(A) through routes;

(B) joint fares; and

(C) divisions of tariffs, subject to approval of a tariff-regulating body that has jurisdiction.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 5
### Chapter 172. Rural Rail Transp. Districts
##### § 172.154 — Agreements with Other Entities for Joint Use

A district may:

    (1) enter into agreements with a public utility, private utility, communication system, common carrier, or transportation system for the joint use of its facilities, installations, or property inside or outside the district; and

    (2) establish:

        (A) through routes;

        (B) joint fares; and

        (C) divisions of tariffs, subject to approval of a tariff-regulating body that has jurisdiction.

*Added by Acts 2009, 81st Leg., R.S., Ch. 85 (S.B.1540), Sec. 2.04, eff. April 1, 2011.*

subsections.text: enter into agreements with a public utility, private utility, communication system, common carrier, or transportation system for the joint use of its facilities, installations, or property inside or outside the district; and

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: divisions of tariffs, subject to approval of a tariff-regulating body that has jurisdiction.
